events.title: Take a Spin at LeMay
events.loc: LeMay – America’s Car Museum , 2702 E. D St., Tacoma
events.desc: If you’ve ever visited LeMay, you’ve likely daydreamed about taking a spin in one of the cars from the museum’s collections. That dream can become a reality this weekend, with a spin around Haub Family Field. Make a day of it wandering the museum exhibits, and plan for lunch at the new café.

events.title: Fircrest Fun Days
events.loc: Fircrest Park , 555 Contra Costa Ave., Fircrest
events.desc: Travel back in time this weekend to the Old West with this themed festival. There will be live concerts, artist and vendor booths, plenty of food trucks, and a couple of bake-offs. You can also expect story time, face painting and interactive activities in the Kidz Zone. Plus, there will be fireworks on Saturday!

events.title: GeekFest West
events.desc: Considered the largest geek event in Snohomish County, GeekFest West has something for everyone. There will be indoor and outdoor entertainment ranging from cosplay and LARPing to chess, video games and pinball. Sunday’s events are especially geared toward families, with children’s cosplay demos, video game and arcade tournaments, tutorials and more.

events.title: Kla Ha Ya Days
events.loc: Historic Downtown Snohomish , 1024 First St., Snohomish
events.desc: I always love spending a day in downtown Snohomish — from a perfect brunch to excellent shopping — but it really comes to life during this annual summer fest. Kla Ha Ya Days is a longstanding tradition with over 100 years of history. Friday will have a special kids fair and outdoor movie, while Saturday brings a pancake breakfast, street fair, parade and circus performances. Don’t miss the car …

events.title: Family Fun Day at Highline SeaTac Botanical Garden
events.loc: Highline SeaTac Botanical Garden , 13735 24th Ave. S., SeaTac
events.desc: What kid wouldn’t be amazed by seeing someone walk by on stilts? This garden fest will not only have stilt walking, but also live music and more entertainment for families. Try out yoga in the garden, watch special demonstrations, and learn about pollinators and their favorite plants.

events.title: Lavender Festival
events.loc: JB Family Growers , 14063 N.E. 145th St., Woodinville
events.desc: I look forward to lavender being in bloom every summer! While I’d love to make the trek out to Sequim , this lavender festival on the Eastside is closer to home and just as delightful. Fields filled with 15,000 lavender plants are in full bloom (and sunflowers are starting to show their faces, too). Sip on lavender lemonade, walk the fields and shop from local vendors.

events.title: SandBlast Festival of the Arts
events.loc: McCormick Park , 26200 N.E. Stephens St., Duvall
events.desc: Artists carve incredible sculptures from sand on the beach at this two-day festival. I am in awe of what they have created at past events (maybe because my family has only ever been able to build lopsided sand castles on the beach). Get inspired by the sand carvers at work, admire local artwork and enjoy the live music.

events.title: Downtown Seattle Block Party
events.desc: It’s no secret that I love a shopping day, and the Nordstrom Anniversary Sale is coming up. It all kicks off with this downtown block party, complete with kid-friendly activities, games, live entertainment and more. The Mariner Moose and Blitz the Seahawk will be making appearances, too!

events.title: Seattle Bon Odori Festival
events.loc: Seattle Betsuin Buddhist Temple , 1427 S. Main St., Seattle
events.desc: Thrilling taiko drumming performances, Japanese folk dancing and more festivities await at this incredible summer celebration honoring ancestral spirits. While you’re there, you can try delicious foods (I can never resist shaved ice) and shop the delightful thrift store. Grown-ups can plan for a pit stop at the beer garden — don’t miss the lively tunes from local reggae band Two Story Zori.

events.title: Parade of Nations and Watch Party
events.loc: Rainier Playfields , 3700 S. Alaska St., Seattle
events.desc: The thrilling World Cup comes to an end this weekend with a final match at noon on Sunday. Celebrate beforehand with a parade that highlights diversity and unity across cultures. There will also be soccer-themed activities and more. Plan for lunch from a food truck!
